meaning: The golden-brown foam on top of a fresh espresso shot, formed by emulsified oils and trapped gases.
Steamed milk with very small, velvety bubbles used for latte art and smooth milk texture.
A structured tasting method used to evaluate aroma, flavor, body, acidity, and aftertaste in coffee.
The process of dissolving flavor compounds from coffee grounds into water during brewing.
The perceived weight and texture of coffee in the mouth, often described as light, medium, or full.
